days.promo_headline: 3 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: X 推出托管式 MCP，AI 直接接入实时平台数据
days.lead_deck: 这不是又一个“接个 API”新闻，而是把 X 的实时信息流包装成模型可直接消费的标准接口。对做搜索、舆情、社媒增长、内容分发的产品来说，信息获取门槛会明显下降，关键变成“谁先把检索、筛选、行动链路接起来”。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI 自研芯片瞄准推理成本砍半
days.lead_deck: 如果“训练模型”继续卷不出差异，真正的战场会转到推理成本和算力议价权。自研芯片一旦落地，产品定价、毛利结构、以及大模型创业公司的成本护城河都会被重写。

days.promo_headline: 3 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google 继续把「好 SEO」和「好 GEO」绑在一起
days.lead_deck: 重点不是把内容写得像机器喂出来的，而是把人真正关心的问题讲清楚；这意味着 AI 搜索时代，内容质量、结构和可信度还是底层资产。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: PloyAI 出来讲的是“让网站自己干活”的全栈营销平台
days.lead_deck: 它把内容、转化、自动化放到一层里卖，说明营销工具正在从“辅助写作”往“直接替你获客”走，后面拼的是闭环效果，不是单点功能。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI 说“代理正在改变工作方式”
days.lead_deck: 这不是单个功能升级，而是把 Codex 变成公司内部的工作层；对产品团队来说，重点不再只是“能不能自动化”，而是“能不能直接接管流程”。

days.promo_headline: 3 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google 推进 AMIE 医疗研究，从“会诊断”走向“会管理”
days.lead_deck: 这不是单点问答能力，而是把模型放进长期病程管理里，意味着医疗场景开始考验持续推理、随访和责任边界；对产品侧的启发是，真正值钱的不是回答，而是把模型嵌进流程。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI 继续把重心往“安全修复自动化”上推
days.lead_deck: 它不是单纯发功能，而是在把漏洞发现、验证、修复、合并做成一条更短的链路；这会直接影响开源维护、企业安全团队和开发者工具的产品形态。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI 继续把“对齐”往长任务场景推进
days.lead_deck: 它不只是模型变聪明，而是在解决模型做更久、更复杂事情时还能保持稳定、安全的行为，这会直接影响 agent、自动化工作流和企业部署。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: 谷歌把医疗 AI 往“长期管理”推进了一步
days.lead_deck: 这不只是演示能力，而是在看 AI 能不能持续跟踪病情、辅助日常管理；对做产品的人来说，核心不是更会聊天，而是能不能嵌进高频、低容错的工作流。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: AI 口号正在失灵
days.lead_deck: TechCrunch 的调查提到，六成美国消费者看到品牌文案里写“AI”会反感。这说明现在不是“把 AI 挂嘴上”就能加分，产品和营销都要回到真实效率、结果和体验。

days.promo_headline: 5 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: State Farm 的销售团队 AI 计划引发强烈反弹
days.lead_deck: 这类“用 AI 先替代人、再优化流程”的动作，最容易在一线组织里炸锅；对产品和增长团队来说，关键不是把 AI 塞进流程，而是先处理利益分配和可解释性。

days.promo_headline: 3 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google AI 五月更新
days.lead_deck: 这类官方更新通常能看出产品节奏和主线方向，重点是搜索、工作流和开发者工具怎么继续被 Gemini 绑在一起。

days.promo_headline: 5 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI 把 Codex 放进 ChatGPT 手机端，开发者能在手机上继续审批、查看和推进长任务
days.lead_deck: 这说明 agent 正从“桌面工作流”往“随时可接管”演进，产品竞争不只拼模型，也拼任务接力体验。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google 和 Arm 把推理继续往设备端推
days.lead_deck: LiteRT、XNNPACK、KleidiAI、SME2 这套组合，说明很多场景不必死等大 GPU，CPU 也能把体验和成本往下压。对手机、笔记本、边缘设备上的产品落地很关键。

days.promo_headline: 4 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: 绿的谐波（LeaderDrive）被拿来当做人形机器人供应链里的关键零部件标的
days.lead_deck: 这条不是在讲“机器人概念”，而是在讲关节减速器、丝杠、电机这些真正会卡量产的物理瓶颈；如果人形机器人放量，最先受益的往往不是最会讲故事的整机厂，而是认证难、替换难、批量一致性要求高的核心零件供应商。

days.promo_headline: 11 signals. 2 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google Flow：Gemini Omni 允许你直接用自然语言改视频
days.lead_deck: 这把视频创作从时间轴操作变成对话式工作流，内容生产门槛会继续下降。

days.promo_headline: 3 signals. 0 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI 把 Codex 放进 ChatGPT 手机端
days.lead_deck: AI 编码代理开始从“只能坐在桌面前管”变成“手机上也能随时接手”，这会直接影响开发者工具的产品形态和工作流设计；后续重点看任务确认、权限控制和长任务接力怎么做。

days.promo_headline: 11 signals. 2 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Hermes 桌面版把 agent 变成操作界面
days.lead_deck: 这说明 agent 正从网页功能往桌面常驻层迁移，分发入口、长任务管理和人机协同会一起改写。

days.promo_headline: 11 signals. 2 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google / Arm: Google and Arm are showing that AI inference can move onto ordin
days.lead_deck: Google and Arm are showing that AI inference can move onto ordinary CPUs with LiteRT, XNNPACK, KleidiAI, and SME2, and the demo is tied to a real Stable Audio model instead of a toy benchmark. That matters because it turns “wait for the right accelerator” into a weaker excuse; product teams now have a credible path to ship useful AI on the hardware people already own.

days.promo_headline: 11 signals. 2 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google / Arm: CPU 侧 AI 正在变得实用
days.lead_deck: 他们把 LiteRT、XNNPACK、KleidiAI 和 SME2 串起来，在普通 CPU 上跑量化后的 Stable Audio 模型，说明 useful inference 正在下沉到设备端，而不是继续只依赖特殊加速器。

days.promo_headline: 11 signals. 2 moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: AI social-card Skill: 歸藏的社交媒体卡片 Skill 冲到 GitHub 本周新建项目榜首
days.lead_deck: 这说明“可复用的 agent 能力”正在从代码资产变成分发资产，AI 工具开始被当作能帮别人更快出内容的基础设施。

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Claude Opus 4.8 ships with Dynamic Workflows and effort control
days.lead_deck: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8 with two features that materially change how agents operate: Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code, which plans work, spawns parallel sub-agents, verifies outputs, and can migrate codebases of hundreds of thousands of lines — currently in research preview on Enterprise, Team, and Max plans. The second is effort control, which lets users tune the token burn rate per task. CursorBench testers reported Opus 4.8 achieved the same output as Opus 4.7 with fewer tool steps, and one tester noted cost parity with GPT-5.5 on internal benchmarks.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Higgsfield AI puts generation directly inside Premiere Pro
days.lead_deck: Higgsfield launched plugins for Adobe Premiere Pro and After Effects that generate images, videos, and transitions inside the timeline — draw on a clip to specify what you want, reframe aspect ratios without cropping, remove backgrounds from footage, and upscale the final export to 4K. With over 624K views and strong reposts from professional editors, it is the clearest sign yet that AI video generation is not competing with professional tools; it is being absorbed into them.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI puts Codex on your phone
days.lead_deck: OpenAI is pushing Codex into the ChatGPT mobile app, so developers can approve decisions, kick off work, and monitor long-running tasks without being chained to a laptop. That matters because coding agents are starting to behave like persistent workers rather than interactive chatbots, and mobile control makes that workflow feel operational instead of experimental.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: AI can now make a living homepage in minutes
days.lead_deck: Vincent Logic’s workflow turns a static website into a page with motion and atmosphere by chaining Pinterest, Gemini, Kling, Mux, and an AI site builder. It is worth watching because it shows how quickly a polished homepage can be assembled once image cleanup, motion, and publishing are all treated as AI steps instead of manual craft.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Gemini Omni brings video editing into chat
days.lead_deck: Google is opening Gemini Omni so people can edit video by describing changes in plain language, and the model is now showing up in Flow with free daily access. That matters because it turns video editing from a timeline skill into an AI-native conversation, which should expand who can ship polished motion content.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: XHS opening Skill uploads could change app distribution
days.lead_deck: A post on X shows Xiaohongshu allowing direct Skill uploads, which sounds like a small feature but points at a much larger platform shift. If social apps start accepting reusable agent capabilities as native objects, distribution stops being about posts alone and becomes about shipping actions inside the feed itself.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Browser skills are turning into the new app store
days.lead_deck: Hermes users are talking about Browse.sh as a place where hundreds of browser skills can be discovered and installed with a single CLI step, and the signal is bigger than the directory itself. It suggests that agent capability is starting to look like a package ecosystem: if a browser task can be shipped as a skill, distribution becomes part of the product surface.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Database-native agents are shedding the dashboard layer
days.lead_deck: Pro Human's new digest argues for agents with full SQL and MCP support, plus direct access to many databases, instead of a dashboard-first abstraction. The important signal is that more AI products are moving closer to the system of record, where the value is speed, control, and fewer layers between intent and action.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Anthropic doubles down on HTML for Claude Code
days.lead_deck: Anthropic is pushing Claude Code output toward HTML instead of Markdown, arguing that richer structure makes plans, reports, and temporary edits easier to read and act on. The bigger signal is that agent UX is becoming a document problem as much as a model problem: the winning interface may be the one humans can scan, trust, and hand back to the agent fastest.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Google and Arm push AI inference onto CPUs
days.lead_deck: Google and Arm are showing how LiteRT, XNNPACK, KleidiAI, and Arm's SME2 can speed up AI inference directly on CPUs, using Stability AI's stable-audio-open-small as the demo path. The important part is not just the benchmark win; it is the strategic bet that useful AI will increasingly run on mainstream hardware instead of waiting for every device to have a special accelerator.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Open Design is turning template packs into an AI moat
days.lead_deck: Tom Huang says html-anything's template pack has now landed across Open Design, and the pitch is bigger than a shortcut library. If agents can produce elite-looking HTML from reusable skills and templates, the moat shifts from raw generation to encoded taste, which is exactly where creator tools start to compound.

days.promo_headline: Eleven signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: Codex + MagicPath could replace Figma faster than expected
days.lead_deck: Dingyi is arguing that Codex paired with MagicPath can cover a surprising amount of the design-to-build loop, making Figma feel less like the center of gravity and more like a handoff layer. The interesting part is not the demo itself but the workflow implication: if prototypes can become shippable interactions with less translation, product teams can collapse days of back-and-forth into one tighter loop.

days.promo_headline: Five signals. Two moves. One pattern.
days.lead_headline: OpenAI puts Codex on mobile, not just desktop
days.lead_deck: OpenAI is pushing coding agents into the phone workflow so tasks can keep moving off desk. This matters because mobile approval and task handoff reduce the friction that slows long-running agent work.

days.promo_headline: Friday set-up — what will matter Monday.
days.lead_headline: Codex on mobile begins to leak
days.lead_deck: Screenshots and a partial App Store listing show OpenAI testing a Codex companion on iOS. This matters because mobile coding agents are the surface that decides where the rest of the year is fought.

days.promo_headline: Marketing day. Receipts from Lenny, Chen, and a Shopify case.
days.lead_headline: Lenny: 6 months of AI in PM workflows
days.lead_deck: Lenny's newsletter published a six-month report on which AI patterns stuck in real PM workflows. This matters because most PM tooling reviews are pre-launch theater, and a six-month retrospective is the rarer signal.

days.promo_headline: Agents week peaks — Anthropic, OpenAI, Google all ship something.
days.lead_headline: Claude can hold a screen for an hour
days.lead_deck: Anthropic extended Claude computer-use to one-hour autonomous sessions with periodic check-in approvals. This matters because long-running computer-use is the line between demo and dependable, and Anthropic just crossed it.

days.promo_headline: Alignment research, two receipts, one Vercel ship.
days.lead_headline: Anthropic: a follow-up to constitutional AI
days.lead_deck: Anthropic posted a follow-up paper extending constitutional AI with model-written critique chains. This matters because the critique-chain mechanism is what governance teams will point to when arguing about audits.

days.promo_headline: Monday hits the gas — Replit, Notion, and a Shopify push.
days.lead_headline: Replit Agents v2 launches with a single-pane runner
days.lead_deck: Replit shipped Agents v2 with a unified runner and per-step approval. This matters because Replit is the cleanest reference UI for agent runs and competitors will copy within a month.

days.promo_headline: Quiet Sunday. Three essays, two model updates.
days.lead_headline: Every: the workspace agent essay
days.lead_deck: Every published a long piece on what a workspace-level agent does that an in-app one cannot. This matters because the workspace surface is the real product battle of the next two quarters.

days.promo_headline: Growth on Saturday: Chen, Haines, and a Latent Space episode.
days.lead_headline: Andrew Chen: PLG is now PLG-with-agents
days.lead_deck: Andrew Chen argues PLG funnels are reshaping into agent-onboarding flows where the agent does the first job. This matters because the funnel diagram every B2B team draws on a whiteboard is about to look different.

days.promo_headline: Payments, edge, and a Linear AI mode.
days.lead_headline: Stripe ships an AI-first payments dashboard
days.lead_deck: Stripe replaced the legacy dashboard search and rules editor with conversational equivalents. This matters because Stripe sets the bar for financial UX and competitors will be pressured to follow within a quarter.

days.promo_headline: Compute deals, new launches, a quiet alignment paper.
days.lead_headline: Anthropic strikes a compute deal with SpaceX
days.lead_deck: Anthropic signed a multi-year capacity arrangement with SpaceX for Starlink-backed compute. This matters because lab capacity is the binding constraint right now and the new pact reshuffles who can train at what scale.
